schema_notes: concept-level booleans with evidence pointers (must-hit findings)

analysis_workbook.formula_expectation: MoM revenue change and share-of-total revenue should be formulas, and Callouts should point back to calculation cells.

topic_dimensions: average order value / order count perspective
forward-looking takeaway / actionable insight for next quarter
ground_truth.callout_notes: peak_month_total_revenue_usd is the all-store November total (UK 1970772.86 + EIRE 47943.37 + Netherlands 55710.75 = 2074426.98). peak_month_top_store_share_pct is UK's share of that all-store total (~95.00%). uk_country_share_pct is UK's share of the full-year all-store total (~92.95%). Posts that cite the UK-only November figure 1,970,772.86 also satisfy peak_month_total_revenue_usd only if they make clear it is UK November and pair it with the all-store November total elsewhere; otherwise this callout should be marked missed.
ground_truth.regional_callout_notes: second_country_share_pct = EIRE share of total revenue (~4.14%; EIRE is the 2nd-largest country after UK). eire_peak_month = month with EIRE's highest monthly revenue (2010-01 at $100,824.27; verified directly from CSV — previous GT value 2010-11 was incorrect). nl_monthly_share_pct = Netherlands share of total revenue (~2.91%). Tolerance is +-1.0 percentage points (absolute) for share values; eire_peak_month must match exactly (2010-01 / January 2010 / Jan 2010 accepted).
